resources.string: Turn BT on, check your BT module name set in options and make sure it\'s paired
Failed to connect with SIO2BT. Try again...

AspeQt needs access to files on your phone so you can pick .atr / .cas / .xex images from any folder (Download, your own Atari folder, etc.).

Android requires this kind of access to be enabled manually in system Settings — there is no Allow/Deny popup for it.

Tap OK to open Settings now. Just toggle on "Allow access to manage all files" and come back. You only need to do this once.
OK — file picker will only see /Android/data/.../files/. You can enable full access later in Android Settings → Apps → AspeQt.
Grant USB permission, then click Start again
